description: AUP not yet in force — Annex A draft exists (docs/SOC2_READINESS.md §27) but has not been signed by the founder and is not distributed to any contractors. No signed copy on file.
root_cause: Pre-launch state: AUP authored but signing step not yet executed; no contractor or employee to distribute to during solo-founder phase

criterion: CC2.3 — External communication to users and regulators
description: No formal board or advisory committee receiving CC2.2 internal control communications with governance accountability. Advisory board documented in docs/ADVISORY_BOARD.md is informal with no fiduciary duty. Investor observer rights planned but not yet agreed.
root_cause: Pre-seed company stage: formal board formation deferred to Seed round close; compensating control is advisory board + investor observer quarterly update

criterion: CC3.2 — Risks to objectives identified and analyzed
description: No standalone signed risk appetite statement document exists as an auditor exhibit. Risk appetite is stated in §14.1 prose but auditors expect a discrete dated founder-signed artifact filed separately from the risk register body.
root_cause: Design gap: content exists in §14.1 but was never extracted into a standalone signed document; identified during §31 CC3 mapping exercise

description: Fraud risk assessment is implicit in §14 (CR-01 cross-tenant bypass and IR-01 harmful AI output) but neither entry is explicitly labeled as a fraud risk for CC3.3 purposes. No dedicated fraud risk assessment table with explicit fraud scenario enumeration likelihood/impact scoring and named detective/preventive controls.
root_cause: Design gap: §14 risk register covers fraud scenarios but does not use CC3.3-required framing; identified during §31 mapping exercise

criterion: CC3.4 — Changes impacting internal controls identified
description: No formal change-impact assessment checklist is triggered by significant organizational changes (new engineering hire new regulatory jurisdiction new market entry acquisition of significant third-party tool). PR-level CC8 controls govern code changes only; organizational and environmental changes are a distinct CC3.4 requirement not fully covered.
root_cause: Design gap: §21 CC8 change management focused on code/deployment changes; organizational change dimension of CC3.4 not addressed until §31 mapping exercise

description: No formal control deficiency log artifact existed prior to this entry. LINEAR tickets served as the operational mechanism for tracking control failures but provided no structured register with mandatory fields (criterion ID deficiency description severity root cause remediation status closed date auditor exhibit reference).
root_cause: Design gap: operational workflow (Linear) not paired with a durable auditor-legible artifact; identified during §32 CC4 mapping exercise; this CSV is the remediation artifact itself

description: Monitoring evidence is not yet in steady state. The product is pre-launch; most CC4.1 ongoing evaluation controls are designed and configured but have not yet accumulated the execution evidence (monthly dashboard screenshots chain integrity event logs access review artifacts) that an auditor expects to see across the 6-month observation window.
root_cause: Structural: pre-launch company cannot accumulate observation-period evidence before launch; gap resolves automatically at launch + 6 months; evidence-collection-plan.md (CC4-E-002) documents the collection schedule

description: No board or audit committee exists to receive deficiency communications as required by CC4.2. AICPA explicitly requires communication to senior management and the board. Compensating control is a three-tier structure: (1) founder self-review in monthly evidence memo (2) advisory board notification of P0/P1 deficiencies at quarterly call (3) investor observer deficiency summary in quarterly update.
root_cause: Structural: solo-founder pre-seed company has not yet formed a formal board; accepted compensating control documented in deficiency-communication-procedure.md (CC4-E-003); must be upgraded to formal audit committee within 90 days of Series A close
